title: calliandra harrisii
intro: Calliandra harrisii, also known as Pink Powder Puff or Harris' powderpuff, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. It is a deciduous shrub or small tree that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is known for its vibrant pink or red flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Calliandra harrisii can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, offering potential health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting immune function, while also being used to treat digestive issues and skin conditions.
